Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: audit fixes — time budget enforcement, import-time side effects, color escapes

- Round test/retry timeouts now derived from remaining wall-clock time
  via _remaining_seconds(), preventing rounds from exceeding their
  stated max_time_minutes budget
- Retry loop bails early when time budget exhausted (<=60s remaining)
- Signal handlers and atexit registration moved from module-level to
  _cleanup.activate(), called only when pipeline() runs — imports are
  now side-effect-free and don't clobber pytest's signal handling
- Color codes (RED, NC) now consistently based on stdout TTY detection;
  err() uses private _err_red/_err_nc for stderr

commit.message:
Redesign export system for homeowner lead lists

- data/homeowner_client.py (new): get_homeowners_in_zone() generates
  mock homeowner records (name, address, phone, email, roof age, home
  value, owner-occupied, storm context); capped at 200 per zone;
  deterministic seed for reproducibility; TODO comment block for real
  provider integration (ATTOM, BatchSkipTrace, DataTree)

- components/export.py: rewrite build_leads_df() to accept homeowner
  df; pdf_bytes() is now a professional lead report (storm summary,
  lead stats, homeowner table up to 100 rows); push_to_ghl() sends
  one GHL contact per homeowner with tags and custom fields

- components/zone_panel.py: both panels call get_homeowners_in_zone()
  after computing the zone; show "Homeowners Identified" stat row;
  CSV/PDF wired to homeowner data; GHL push sends homeowner contacts;
  radius panel uses most-severe event for storm context + aggregate
  home count

- components/filters.py: remove leads_csv param from
  render_sidebar_footer() and drop the download button entirely

- app.py: remove build_leads_df/csv_bytes imports; update
  render_sidebar_footer() call (no leads_csv arg)

fix: C# Hand aggregate raise validation and event emission

Fix playoff odds merging two leagues together

Simulation results were using LEAGUE_TEAMS (mock data) while the
championship favorite card used activeTeams (real data), causing two
different leagues to show simultaneously.

Now all playoff odds simulation data is derived from activeTeams with
rank-based projected odds (makePlayoffs/firstRoundBye/winChamp).
Also replaced hardcoded "Armchair Football League" with real league name.

Feat: add Transactions to borrower mobile bottom navigation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: grip calibration uses max(base, observed) instead of blind replace

Previously, apply_calibration_to_params replaced equipment/default grip
limits with driver-observed values, even when the driver hadn't fully
explored the car's capability. This made the optimal lap time prediction
far too conservative (e.g. 1:45 vs actual potential of ~1:40 at Barber).

Now each axis uses max(base, observed) so calibration can only RAISE
the grip envelope above the base parameters, never lower it. A driver
pulling 0.95G in a 1.15G-capable car will see predictions based on
1.15G (equipment spec or defaults), not their current usage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix login on Streamlit Cloud: replace JS cookie/reload with st.rerun()

components.html() cookies are blocked by third-party restrictions on Cloud,
and window.parent.location.reload() destroys the WebSocket session_state.
Using st.rerun() keeps session_state intact across reruns.

commit.message:
feat: overseer multi-turn e2e test harness (#266)

* feat: add Docker sandbox for overseer e2e tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add aiohttp dependency for overseer report server

* feat: add stream-json parser for overseer e2e tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add session driver for overseer e2e tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add live report server for overseer dashboard

* feat: add overseer LLM for turn analysis and prompt generation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add overseer main script wiring everything together

* style: format and lint overseer modules

* fix: reuse Anthropic client across turns, warn on malformed LLM response

* fix: escape HTML in dashboard to prevent XSS from LLM output

* fix: support compose project isolation for multi-stack environments

Add --compose-project flag to CLI and compose_project parameter to
SessionDriver so docker compose commands target the correct stack.
Change ensure_sandbox_running() to verify status instead of managing
lifecycle, avoiding port conflicts with other running stacks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add --sandbox-model flag to control Claude Code model

Defaults to claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 for cost-effective testing.
Pass --sandbox-model claude-sonnet-4-6 for more capable sessions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add --passthrough flag for real credential auth

When set, uses ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from env as the sandbox credential
so requests authenticate through the proxy via passthrough/both mode
instead of the proxy API key.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: make passthrough auth the default

Passthrough mode (using ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from env) is now the default.
Use --no-passthrough to fall back to proxy API key authentication.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: graceful timeout handling and stale process cleanup

- Make TimeoutError non-fatal: returns TurnSummary with anomaly instead
  of crashing the overseer loop
- Add _kill_stale_claude_processes() to pkill orphaned claude processes
  inside the sandbox container after a timeout
- Remove passthrough auth flags (Claude Code CLI doesn't support real
  API keys with custom base URLs)
- Increase default turn timeout to 600s

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: support OAuth token passthrough for sandbox auth

SessionDriver now accepts auth_token (OAuth) as an alternative to
api_key. When auth_token is set, it's passed as ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN
to the sandbox container, which Claude Code sends as
Authorization: Bearer — forwarded by the proxy directly to Anthropic.

This means sandbox sessions use the caller's existing Anthropic org
billing instead of a separate API key.

main.py defaults to ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN from env if available,
falling back to proxy key auth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback

- Add defensive handling for empty LLM responses in overseer_llm.py
- Fix SSE broadcast race condition by iterating a copy of the queue list
- Bind dashboard to 127.0.0.1 instead of 0.0.0.0
- Mask credentials in session driver log output
- Break long lines in _kill_stale_claude_processes
- Add unit tests for CLI argument parsing (test_overseer_main.py)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address additional review feedback

- Remove unused overseer_analysis param from add_turn()
- Handle BrokenPipeError in SSE client disconnect
- Stop logging partial API keys, just log "API key (set)"
- Consistent logger naming across overseer modules

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: format overseer files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: route cron jobs to sfo1 (near Supabase us-west-2) and add multi-region to DB-heavy APIs

Supabase is in us-west-2 (Oregon), Vercel defaults to hnd1 (Tokyo).
Cron jobs don't serve users — route them to sfo1 to minimize DB latency (~20ms vs ~150ms).
User-facing DB-heavy routes (rankings, search, compare) get multi-region [sfo1, hnd1].

commit.message:
Fase 8.D fix: compensar crop offset en sprites de personajes/objetos

Problema: BlitRenderTextureToDDraw aplica crop (sModX,sModY) para
corregir tiles, pero ese crop tambien desplazaba personajes/arboles/
objetos que usan coordenadas de pantalla (no PDBGS).

Solucion:
- Agregar GetCropX()/GetCropY() a IRenderBackend
- En PutSpriteFast (Sprite.cpp + Sprite2.cpp): sumar cropOffset
  a la posicion SFML de personajes/objetos. Esto compensa el crop
  que se aplica en EndFrame, dejandolos en la posicion correcta.
- PutSpriteFastNoColorKeyDst (tiles) NO se modifica: sus posiciones
  ya incluyen sModX/sModY (coordenadas PDBGS) y el crop las corrige.

Resultado: tiles se recortan correctamente, personajes/objetos
quedan fijos en pantalla independiente del scroll.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix launch scraper: restore data and rewrite for prose parsing

The previous scraper parsed the wrong Wikipedia table (casualties instead
of launches). Wikipedia has no structured launch table — data is in prose.

Rewritten scraper now:
- Parses daily sections (=== 28 February ===) for missile/drone mentions
- Only adds genuinely new days with nonzero launch data
- Preserves existing curated data and targets
- Exits cleanly without changes if scrape fails or finds nothing new

Initial implementation of Winnow - Revenue Pipeline Validator

This commit includes the complete implementation of Tickets 1-3:

## Ticket 1: Repository Scaffolding
- Set up Turborepo monorepo with 4 packages: core, api, web, mock-data
- Configured TypeScript strict mode across all packages
- Set up NestJS backend with health endpoint
- Set up React + Vite + Tailwind CSS frontend
- Defined Prisma schema for PostgreSQL
- Configured GitHub Actions CI pipeline
- All packages build and test successfully

## Ticket 2: Mock Data Generator
- Implemented 6 pre-built scenarios (healthy, problematic, unrealistic-dates, thin-committees, stale-pipeline, mixed)
- Generated realistic deal data with proper distributions
- Implemented seniority level derivation from job titles
- Created CLI tool for generating mock data
- Ensured chronological consistency across all date fields
- 21 tests covering all scenarios and edge cases

## Ticket 3: Benchmark Computation Service
- Implemented BenchmarkService for analyzing closed-won deal history
- Statistical utilities: median, percentile, segmentize functions
- Cycle length analysis by deal size segments
- Stage time distribution computation
- Contact count percentiles by deal size
- Confidence level calculation (high/medium/low)
- Automatic fallback to industry benchmarks when insufficient data
- 21 comprehensive tests covering all scenarios and edge cases

All 45 tests passing across all packages.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(geocode): fix Nominatim missing city for non-municipality Chinese cities

Nominatim often puts the district (区/县) in the `city` field for
provincial cities like Hangzhou and Chengdu, while the actual city name
(杭州市, 成都市) only appears in `display_name`. The existing fix only
handled 直辖市 (municipalities with empty state field).

Add a second inference pass that detects when `city` looks like a
district name (ends in 区/县), then extracts the real city from
display_name by finding the segment ending in 市.

Before: 拱墅区天水街道 / 武侯区桂溪街道 / 浦东新区（东岸绿道）
After:  杭州市拱墅区（天水街道） / 成都市武侯区（桂溪街道） / 上海市浦东新区东岸绿道

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
